title: Gradients
lede: KIMES gradients are a digital-only auxiliary surface treatment. They appear on web hero sections, app backgrounds, video intros, and animated SNS thumbnails. Gradients are forbidden on print materials, logos, typography, and brand-color application — the flat-graphic identity of KIMES print remains unchanged.

set.title: 05.1 — Brand gradient set
set.desc: One official gradient per brand. All KIMES gradients are 2-stop linear at 135°. Variations in angle, stop count, or color outside this set are not approved.

applications.title: 05.2 — Application examples
applications.desc: How each gradient appears in a typical web-hero context. Treat the gradient as a backdrop for white logo + headline + CTA — never as the logo itself.

contexts.title: 05.3 — Allowed contexts
contexts.desc: Gradients are reserved for digital surfaces with reliable color reproduction. Print, signage, and logo applications stay flat.

donts.title: 05.4 — Don'ts
donts.desc: Four common mistakes. Stay within the official gradient set.

donts.label: 3+ colors
donts.desc: Use 2-color gradients only — no rainbow stops.

donts.label: Radial gradient
donts.desc: Linear at 135° only. No radial, conic, or mesh.

donts.label: Gradient on type
donts.desc: Type stays solid — never fill text with a gradient.

donts.label: Gradient on logo
donts.desc: Logos always render in a single brand color.
